package com.example.service;

import org.apache.commons.io.IOUtils;

import java.io.IOException;
import java.net.URI;
import java.net.URISyntaxException;
import java.util.Arrays;
import java.util.HashSet;
import java.util.Set;

/**
 * A service that provides remote resource fetching functionality.
 */
public class RemoteResourceService {

    // Define allowed domains and protocols
    private static final Set<String> ALLOWED_DOMAINS = new HashSet<>(Arrays.asList(
        "www.aliyun.com",
        "aliyun.com"
    ));
    
    private static final Set<String> ALLOWED_SCHEMES = new HashSet<>(Arrays.asList(
        "https",
        "http"
    ));

    /**
     * Fetches the content from the given URL.
     * This method is used to retrieve and process remote HTTP/HTTPS resources.
     *
     * @param resourceUrl The URL of the resource, e.g., "https://www.aliyun.com/robots.txt"
     * @return The byte array content of the resource. Returns null if an error occurs or the URL is invalid.
     */
    public byte[] getResourceContent(String resourceUrl) {
        if (resourceUrl == null || resourceUrl.trim().isEmpty()) {
            return null;
        }

        try {
            // Validate URL format first
            URI uri = new URI(resourceUrl);
            
            // Validate scheme
            String scheme = uri.getScheme();
            if (scheme == null || !ALLOWED_SCHEMES.contains(scheme.toLowerCase())) {
                System.err.println("Invalid or disallowed URL scheme: " + scheme);
                return null;
            }
            
            // Validate host/domain
            String host = uri.getHost();
            if (host == null || !isDomainAllowed(host)) {
                System.err.println("Domain not allowed: " + host);
                return null;
            }
            
            // Additional security checks
            if (!isSafeURL(resourceUrl)) {
                System.err.println("URL failed security checks");
                return null;
            }

            // Use IOUtils to fetch content from the URI
            return IOUtils.toByteArray(uri);
        } catch (URISyntaxException | IOException e) {
            System.err.println("Failed to fetch resource: " + e.getMessage());
            return null;
        }
    }
    
    /**
     * Checks if the domain is allowed based on whitelist
     */
    private boolean isDomainAllowed(String host) {
        // Remove any trailing dots
        String normalizedHost = host.toLowerCase().replaceAll("\\.+$", "");
        
        // Check exact match
        if (ALLOWED_DOMAINS.contains(normalizedHost)) {
            return true;
        }
        
        // Check for subdomain matches
        for (String allowedDomain : ALLOWED_DOMAINS) {
            if (normalizedHost.endsWith("." + allowedDomain) || normalizedHost.equals(allowedDomain)) {
                return true;
            }
        }
        
        return false;
    }
    
    /**
     * Additional security checks for the URL
     */
    private boolean isSafeURL(String url) {
        // Check for dangerous protocols
        String lowerUrl = url.toLowerCase();
        if (lowerUrl.contains("javascript:") || 
            lowerUrl.contains("data:") || 
            lowerUrl.contains("file:") ||
            lowerUrl.contains("ftp:") ||
            lowerUrl.contains("mailto:")) {
            return false;
        }
        
        // Check for IP address usage (optional - prevents bypassing domain restrictions)
        // This can be enabled if IP addresses should not be used
        // return !isIPAddress(url);
        
        return true;
    }
}

package com.example.service;

import org.apache.commons.io.IOUtils;

import java.io.IOException;
import java.net.InetAddress;
import java.net.Inet6Address;
import java.net.URI;
import java.net.URISyntaxException;
import java.net.UnknownHostException;

/**
 * A service that provides remote resource fetching functionality.
 */
public class RemoteResourceService {

    /**
     * Fetches the content from the given URL.
     * This method is used to retrieve and process remote HTTP/HTTPS resources.
     *
     * @param resourceUrl The URL of the resource, e.g., "https://www.aliyun.com/robots.txt"
     * @return The byte array content of the resource. Returns null if an error occurs or the URL is invalid.
     */
    public byte[] getResourceContent(String resourceUrl) {
        if (resourceUrl == null || resourceUrl.trim().isEmpty()) {
            return null;
        }

        try {
            // Convert the string to a URI object
            URI uri = new URI(resourceUrl);

            // Validate scheme
            String scheme = uri.getScheme();
            if (scheme == null || (!scheme.equalsIgnoreCase("http") && !scheme.equalsIgnoreCase("https"))) {
                return null;
            }

            // Validate host
            String host = uri.getHost();
            if (host == null || !isAllowedHost(host)) {
                return null;
            }

            // Use IOUtils to fetch content from the URI
            return IOUtils.toByteArray(uri);
        } catch (URISyntaxException | IOException e) {
            // In a real application, this would log the error
            System.err.println("Failed to fetch resource: " + e.getMessage());
            return null;
        }
    }

    /**
     * Checks if a host is allowed for remote fetching.
     * Blocks private/internal addresses and localhost.
     *
     * @param host The host to validate
     * @return true if the host is allowed, false otherwise
     */
    private boolean isAllowedHost(String host) {
        try {
            InetAddress address = InetAddress.getByName(host);
            return !address.isLoopbackAddress() 
                && !address.isSiteLocalAddress() 
                && !address.isAnyLocalAddress() 
                && !address.isLinkLocalAddress() 
                && !address.isMulticastAddress() 
                && !isIPv6ULA(address);
        } catch (UnknownHostException e) {
            // Block if unable to resolve host
            return false;
        }
    }

    /**
     * Checks if an address is an IPv6 Unique Local Address (ULA)
     * These addresses (fc00::/7) are private and not routable on the internet
     *
     * @param address The address to check
     * @return true if ULA, false otherwise
     */
    private boolean isIPv6ULA(InetAddress address) {
        if (address instanceof Inet6Address) {
            byte[] bytes = address.getAddress();
            // fc00::/7 range check (first byte has bits 11111100)
            if (bytes.length == 16 && (bytes[0] & 0xfe) == 0xfc) {
                return true;
            }
        }
        return false;
    }
}
